### LOGIN-13 — needs-rework

**Reviewer:** Sam Patel (Security Lead)
**Timestamp:** 2026-04-26T11:42:08Z
**Signature (truncated):** `d2e8c1a6f...`

> TOTP enrollment requires Supabase Enroll toggle (separate from Verify flag) —
> demo-dashboard has no TOTP backend wired. Blocking sign-off until the
> operational runbook documents the required Supabase project config and a real
> TOTP endpoint is wired to the demo. ASVS-2.8.1 and ASVS-2.8.3 cannot be
> asserted in demo mode.

Standards blocked: ASVS-2.8.1 (TOTP Verifier), ASVS-2.8.3 (TOTP Replay Resistance)

**Action required:** Wire Auth.js v5 TOTP adapter (or mock endpoint) to
demo-dashboard, update operational runbook, then re-submit for sign-off.

## What this proves (and doesn't)

**Tamper-evident:** the hash chain in `uat-log.jsonl` cryptographically binds each
entry to the previous one. Each `prev_hash` field is the HMAC-SHA256 of the raw
JSON string of the line above it. Each `signature` field is the HMAC-SHA256 of
`tc_id + "|" + status + "|" + reviewer + "|" + ts`. Modifying any entry's
content, signature, or order breaks the chain — `testnux sign --verify` returns
`{ valid: false, brokenAt: <line> }`. See `src/lib/uat-log.mjs` for the
implementation.

**Reviewer attribution:** each entry records who attested what, when, with what
justification. Required fields (`tc_id`, `status`, `reviewer`, `reviewer_role`,
`ts`, `prev_hash`, `signature`) are enforced at write time by `appendEntry()`.

**NOT court-admissible standalone:** the e-signature here is an audit-trail
mechanism, not a legally binding electronic signature under eIDAS / ESIGN /
UETA. For court-admissible attestations, pair this log with a separate
e-signature workflow (DocuSign, Adobe Sign, etc.). See `SECURITY.md` and
`docs/v0.3-uat.md` for the full legal-disclaimer scope.

---

## Stale-acceptance check

Entries older than 90 days require re-attestation before a release gate can
pass. Earliest entry above: 2026-04-26. Next stale-acceptance review:
**2026-07-25**.

---

## How to add a sign-off entry

```bash
testnux sign demo-dashboard-login
# Interactive prompts: TC ID, status (accepted|rejected|needs-rework|pending),
# reviewer name, role, justification.
# Computes HMAC-SHA256 + appends to uat-log.jsonl + regenerates this markdown.
```

To verify chain integrity at any time:

```bash
testnux sign --verify demo-dashboard-login
# Output: { valid: true, brokenAt: null, errors: [] }
# Or on tamper: { valid: false, brokenAt: 3, errors: ["Line 3 (LOGIN-13): prev_hash mismatch — chain broken. Expected b9e1f3... got 00000000..."] }
```
